knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: Distinguish a durable learning atom from a reusable case pattern: an atom is a compact invariant or rule usable across contexts; a case pattern is an example-shaped template that may guide analogy but is not yet generalized truth.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X needs this boundary so Builder does not promote anecdote, local episode, or lab artifact into active retained knowledge too early.
compact_missing_knowledge: Exact Builder criteria for atom granularity
Allowed fields for retained atoms versus case records
Known failure examples where cases were over-promoted
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare accepted atom examples against diagnostic case records
Write provisional criteria without changing storage
Keep route and runtime claims out of the distinction
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Reviewer can classify mixed examples consistently as atom, case pattern, or open gap
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X1 as the definition layer for deciding what kind of learning object Builder is handling.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: This topic exposes a weakness in compression judgment: the system may either overfit to specific cases or over-abstract into vague atoms that cannot steer behavior.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X must identify why Builder learning needs disciplined promotion instead of treating every useful observation as accepted knowledge.
compact_missing_knowledge: Where current Builder memory loses case context
Where current compact storage loses operational specificity
Whether failures come from abstraction, retrieval, or reuse
compact_safe_learning_steps: Audit one known diagnostic route as a thought exercise
Track which claims need proof before retention
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A failure analysis shows at least one concrete misclassification risk and a mitigation
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X2 to define the risk model that promotion rules must defend against.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: Builder self-build and child-agent build need both forms: atoms for reusable decisions and case patterns for scenario recognition, debugging, and curriculum examples.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X matters because active behavior absorption depends on knowing what should become compact retained guidance versus what should remain indexed evidence or training cases.
compact_missing_knowledge: How decision reuse records cite their source type
compact_safe_learning_steps: Map consumer surfaces conceptually before mutation
Treat case patterns as evidence scaffolds until validated
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A retrieval-to-decision trace can show whether an atom or case pattern was used appropriately
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X3 to connect the taxonomy to actual Builder behavior surfaces.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is valid only if classification improves behavior without inventing authority: examples must classify repeatably, retrieve correctly, and produce measurable decision differences.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X needs proof gates so the distinction is not philosophical decoration but an operational rule for learning promotion.
compact_missing_knowledge: Canonical validator for atom retention versus case retention
compact_validation_needed: Regression check that cases are not treated as accepted atoms
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X4 as the acceptance checklist for any proposed atom/case rule.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: Promotion decides whether a learned item is a case pattern, atom candidate, accepted atom candidate pending proof, or open gap needing more evidence.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: X ends in a routing decision: store as case, compact as atom candidate, reject, or defer as gap without pretending acceptance.
compact_safe_learning_steps: Do not update active memory without explicit validated route
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Each promoted item has source evidence, classification rationale, and proof status
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X5 to turn the analysis into a bounded return recommendation for Builder learning.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 because failure modes require a clear atom/case boundary
X3 depends on X1 and X2 because relevance follows from type and risk
X5 depends on X4 because promotion requires validation gates
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 overlaps X5: validation defines proof; promotion applies it
X2 overlaps X4: both discuss failure, but X2 names risks while X4 tests them

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Rebuild X as a small promotion framework: define atom versus case, name failure risks, map Builder consumers, set validation gates, then return one conservative classification decision path.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Create a read-only classification rubric with 3 labels: case_pattern, atom_candidate, open_gap.
proof_needed: Human or validator classifies sample items consistently
At least one retrieval-to-decision trace shows correct source-type use
No case pattern is promoted to accepted atom without evidence and proof status

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
